title: elymus ciliaris var. lasiophyllus
intro: Elymus ciliaris var. lasiophyllus, also known as a type of wild wheat grass or blue wild rye, is a plant species native to mountainous regions in East Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, Elymus ciliaris var. lasiophyllus is often used as a tea or infused in water to make a medicinal drink.
